children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this chapter, any reference in any statute, reorganization plan, Executive order, regulation, agreement, determination, or other official document or proceeding to the United States International Development Cooperation Agency (IDCA) or to the Director or any other officer or employee of IDCA—
insofar as such reference relates to any function or authority transferred under

children.tail: , shall be deemed to refer to the Secretary of State;

children.text: insofar as such reference relates to any function or authority transferred under

children.tail: , shall be deemed to refer to the Administrator of the Agency for International Development;

children.text: insofar as such reference relates to any function or authority transferred under

children.tail: , shall be deemed to refer to the head of the agency to which such function or authority is transferred under such section; and

children.text: insofar as such reference relates to any function or authority not transferred by this subchapter, shall be deemed to refer to the President or such agency or agencies as may be specified by Executive order.
This chapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this subdivision”, meaning subdiv. A of div. G of

children.tail: , known as the Foreign Affairs Agencies Consolidation Act of 1998. For complete classification of this subdivision to the Code, see Short Title note set out under

children.text: This subchapter, referred to in par. (4), was in the original “this title”, meaning title XIV of subdiv. A of div. G of

children.tail: . For complete classification of title XIV to the Code, see Tables.
